_meta.notes: Five-beat arc for deep-mark in the Coral Anchorage. The Tidehands have requested a piece of reef-iridium that has fallen from the living reef into the deep below the platform. Only the deep-mark can retrieve it — the rest of the navy stops at the depth where the rung-line gives out. The dive is the test. Living coral cannot be cut; the deep-mark takes only what is given. Win: lung>=10 AND depth>=10 at turn>=8 (returned with the find, or returned having decided the depth was the point). Lose: lung<=0 (stayed too long, the surface lost you). The Reef Watcher above keeps the time.

beats.narrative: You stand at the lip of the seaward platform. The water below is the colour of inside-of-the-mouth red, a yellow that is not gold, a sun-orange. The living coral pulses slowly, slowly. The piece the Tidehands want has fallen past it — into the deep where their line stops being able to follow you. The pressure has begun its slow change against your ears. The descent begins now, or it does not begin.

suggestedVerbs.label: begin the descent
suggestedVerbs.description: the seam-line goes down; your hand finds the first rung; the platform recedes above you

suggestedVerbs.label: feel the water's grain
suggestedVerbs.description: before you go, learn the day's pressure; the reef will not be the reef it was yesterday

suggestedVerbs.label: rest one breath
suggestedVerbs.description: the next breath is the breath you take down with you; let it be a full one

beats.narrative: Thirty meters down. The rung-line is your line; the navy laid it years ago and it has held. The water at this depth is colder than the water at the surface and is colder than the water ten meters below. The pressure tells you where you are. The platform's underside is a small bright shape above you. The deep is below.

suggestedVerbs.label: follow the rung-line down
suggestedVerbs.description: hand over hand; the line knows the way; the depth knows you

suggestedVerbs.label: feel the cold change
suggestedVerbs.description: the water at this layer is a different water; the reef's edge is below the cold

suggestedVerbs.label: rest at the line's mid
suggestedVerbs.description: the line allows pauses; the breath holds; the body adjusts

beats.narrative: The rung-line stops at the last rung. Below the last rung is the deep the navy does not go to. The reef-iridium is below the last rung. The water here is the colour of a memory you have of dark. You can feel the world's plates drift in your bones. The platform above is no longer a shape; it is a direction, only.

suggestedVerbs.label: go past the last rung
suggestedVerbs.description: the line ends; you go on; this is the part of the dive only the deep-mark does

suggestedVerbs.label: feel for the iridium
suggestedVerbs.description: the metal calls in a frequency the water carries; locate without seeing

suggestedVerbs.label: listen for the wyrm
suggestedVerbs.description: the deep is wyrm-touched; the older sound is here; locate the iridium by what it is near

beats.narrative: The piece is where the watcher said it would be — fallen from the living reef, lying on a shelf of dead coral, wholly given. You can take it. The reef has not asked you not to. Or you can leave it; the dive itself is the work, and the depth is the point, and the surface knows by your return whether the deep was kind to you. Either is acceptable to the watcher above. Both have been done before.

suggestedVerbs.label: feel the piece
suggestedVerbs.description: weight, shape, edge; the iridium reports its own shape to your hand

suggestedVerbs.label: rest at the find
suggestedVerbs.description: the piece can wait; your breath cannot; the choice can take a beat

suggestedVerbs.label: begin the rise
suggestedVerbs.description: with or without the piece; the dive's true work was the dive; either return is honored

beats.narrative: The platform's underside is a shape again, then a face — the watcher, holding the line. You break the surface. The day is the day you left. The watcher logs the time; they do not ask whether you brought the piece. They will see it on the platform if you have, and not see it if you have not. Either way: the dive is closed. The deep is yours again until tomorrow's slate.

suggestedVerbs.label: complete the surface
suggestedVerbs.description: the dive's last meter; the reef-edge accepts you back

suggestedVerbs.label: rest on the platform
suggestedVerbs.description: the lung returns; the depth subsides; the body is yours again

suggestedVerbs.label: feel the day
suggestedVerbs.description: the surface's pressure is its own pressure; learn it again, slowly
